description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular metal head and a long, straight handle. The head is dark gray, likely forged from steel, and features a central metallic cap or bolt on each striking face, suggesting reinforced construction. The handle is a reddish-brown color, possibly wood or a composite material, and is capped with metallic bands at both ends for added durability. The overall design is utilitarian, built for delivering powerful blows in demolition or construction tasks. The hammer's proportions and materials indicate it is intended for two-handed use, providing significant leverage and impact force. No visible branding, wear, or accessories are present, giving it a clean, well-maintained appearance.
description: A large, heavy sledgehammer with a dark metal head and a long, reddish-brown handle reinforced with metallic bands. Built for strength and durability, it appears clean and unused.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, rectangular face of the metal head and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the hammer head and the central metallic cap, with the handle extending directly downward.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the opposite striking face and the handle's alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the same narrow head profile and visible metallic cap.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a reddish-brown finish, reinforced with metallic bands at both ends, smooth and cylindrical.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head made of dark steel, with flat striking faces and a central metallic cap or bolt on each side.
